sourcePrompt: A UH-1H "Huey" transport helicopter, the workhorse of the US Army in Vietnam, about thirteen meters nose to tail and parked on its skids. A rounded greenhouse cockpit with chin windows flows into a broad slab-sided cabin with wide open side doorways; from there a slim tail boom, longer than the cabin itself, tapers back to a small fin with a tail rotor and short horizontal stabilizers. Two long landing skids ride on tubular cross struts, and a two-bladed main rotor longer than the fuselage sits on a tall mast above the cabin. Olive-drab overall, iconic and utilitarian.
handEdit: Math.PI radian rotations converted to intended degrees (createPart rotation is degrees); offline rebake, no model call
